title: The Merchant of Venice
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: A comedy exploring themes of mercy, justice, and prejudice through the story of Antonio, a merchant who borrows money from Shylock, a Jewish moneylender.
context: Written in Elizabethan England, the play reflects antisemitic attitudes of the time while also challenging them through its complex portrayal of Shylock.

characters.name: Shylock
characters.description: A Jewish moneylender seeking revenge for Antonio's insults. Complex character who is both villain and victim.

characters.name: Portia
characters.description: A wealthy heiress who tests her suitors with the casket test, then disguises herself as a lawyer.

characters.name: Antonio
characters.description: The merchant who borrows from Shylock, risking a pound of flesh.

characters.name: Bassanio
characters.description: Antonio's friend who courts Portia to pay off his debts.

characters.name: Jessica
characters.description: Shylock's daughter who elopes with Lorenzo, taking her father's wealth.

characters.name: Lorenzo
characters.description: A Christian who elopes with Jessica, Shylock's daughter.

themes.name: Prejudice and Antisemitism
themes.description: The treatment of Jews in Christian society.
examples: Antonio's abuse and spitting on Shylock
The way Christians speak about Jews
The 'payment' scene where Shylock demands his bond

themes.name: Mercy
themes.description: The virtue of forgiveness and compassion.
examples: Antonio asking Shylock to show mercy
The mercy shown to Shylock at the end of the trial

themes.name: Justice
themes.description: The law and its application, including poetic justice.
examples: The strict interpretation of Shylock's bond
Portia arguing that mercy supersedes law

themes.name: Appearance vs Reality
themes.description: Things are not always what they seem.
examples: Portia disguising herself as a lawyer
The casket test revealing true character
Bassanio choosing the correct casket based on love

quotes.text: The quality of mercy is not strain'd, it droppeth as the gentle rain from heaven upon the place beneath.
quotes.speaker: Portia
quotes.context: Her famous speech on mercy delivered in the courtroom

quotes.text: If you prick us, do we not bleed? If you tickle us, do we not laugh? If you poison us, do we not die?
quotes.speaker: Shylock
quotes.context: Shylock's powerful speech demanding equal treatment

quotes.text: All that glitters is not gold; often have you heard that told.
quotes.speaker: Arragon
quotes.context: The inscription on the silver casket that saves Portia from unworthy suitors

quotes.text: I am a Jew. Hath not a Jew eyes?
quotes.speaker: Shylock
quotes.context: Shylock's plea for humanity and recognition of shared human experience

quotes.text: So may the outward shows be least themselves; the world is still deceived with ornament.
quotes.speaker: Bassanio
quotes.context: Reflecting on the casket test and the nature of appearances

quotes.text: It is a wise father that knows his own child.
quotes.speaker: Launcelot
quotes.context: Commenting on the relationship between Shylock and Jessica
